decisions.decision: Keep this step test-only in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` and focus the stricter response-shape assertions on the live HTTP health tests, because the route-level tests already cover `/v1/health` dispatch and helper-level tests already freeze the nested component schema.
alternatives_rejected: Reopening `backend/server.py` implementation work for a step whose remaining gap is coverage only
Duplicating the same response-shape assertions across helper, route, and HTTP layers

decisions.decision: Freeze the `/v1/health` HTTP contract as the exact top-level field set `service`, `status`, `timestamp`, `version`, `uptime_seconds`, and `components`, with `components` keyed exactly as `db`, `inference`, and `auth`, while leaving nested component-object field details to the existing helper-level tests.
alternatives_rejected: Only checking that a few additive fields exist without pinning the full public envelope
Repeating the full nested per-component schema assertions at the HTTP boundary

decisions.decision: Treat `uptime_seconds` as a stable public type/shape assertion only at the HTTP boundary by checking that it is a float, and avoid exact-value or precision assertions there because the value depends on live process uptime.
alternatives_rejected: Asserting an exact uptime value in the HTTP test
Promoting helper-level rounding behavior into a brittle end-to-end equality check

decisions.decision: Tighten the unchanged legacy health-route coverage to assert that `/health` and `/healthz` still return the exact three-field payload shape `service`, `status`, and `timestamp`, instead of only proving that some `/v1/health`-only fields are absent.
alternatives_rejected: Keeping the legacy compatibility checks limited to absence of `version`, `uptime_seconds`, and `components`
Comparing the full live legacy payload by literal dict equality despite the dynamic timestamp

steps.task: Update the existing `/v1/health` HTTP test in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` so it asserts the exact top-level key set, the inherited `service`/`status` values and `timestamp` shape, `version == server.ENGINE_CONFIG.version`, `uptime_seconds` being a float, and the exact `components` key set.

Keep the HTTP coverage focused on the public response envelope by avoiding new end-to-end assertions for nested `db`, `inference`, and `auth` subfields that are already frozen by the helper-level tests in the same module.

Tighten the legacy `/health` and `/healthz` HTTP test to assert the exact unchanged three-field payload shape and timestamp formatting, preserving compatibility expectations for existing probes and clients.
Run the focused health-route tests in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`, then review the diff and commit the step-3 test-plan implementation once the planning artifact is in place.
